info.name: ZapUnlocked API (auto-generated)
info.description: Auto-generated from OpenAPI. Base URL: http://localhost:8300

request.description:
Sends a text message to Meta AI via WhatsApp and waits for a response.

The message is forwarded to the Meta AI contact (13135550002@s.whatsapp.net). The server blocks until a reply is received or the `timeout` is reached.

Required: `message` (your question/prompt).
Optional: `timeout` (seconds to wait, default 30).

Response includes:
- `text` — the AI's textual reply
- `hasImage` — whether the response contains an image
- `messageId` — the WhatsApp message ID of the reply

⚠ Requires an active WhatsApp connection.

Generates an image using Meta AI's `/imagine` feature.

Internally sends `/imagine {prompt}` to Meta AI and returns the generated image (along with any accompanying text).

Required: `prompt` — the image description.
Optional: `timeout` (seconds to wait, default 60).

Response shape is the same as /ai/ask, with `hasImage: true` when successful.

⚠ Requires an active WhatsApp connection.
Get Collection.
Update Collection.

Request body: `Contact` schema

Block User.

Request body: `Block` schema

Return device data (Device, RawAgent, Integrator, etc).

Return instance profile data (phone, jid, PushName, etc).

Required: `name` (string).

Get Qr Image.
Logout.

Pairs a new device using a pairing code — an alternative to QR code scanning.

The server requests a pairing code from WhatsApp and displays it. Enter this code on the WhatsApp mobile app under Linked Devices.

Required: `phone` (the phone number to pair, with country code).

Enables or disables automatic read marking for incoming messages. When enabled, messages are marked as read immediately upon receipt.

Required: `value` (boolean).

Enables or disables automatic call rejection. When enabled, incoming WhatsApp calls are automatically declined without ringing the paired device.

Required: `value` (boolean).

Sets the message sent/displayed when a call is automatically rejected.

Required: `value` (string). Only applies when `call-reject-auto` is enabled.

Get Phone Pair Code.

Path parameters: `phone`

Generate a pairing code to connect a number without QR Code.
Enter the code directly in WhatsApp (Linked Devices > Link with phone number).

Requires client to be awaiting connection (no active session).

Deletes a message for everyone (if possible, within WhatsApp's time limit) or just for the bot itself.

Required: `phone`, `messageId`.

Edits an existing text message. Only works for messages sent by the same bot.

Required: `phone`, `messageId`, `message` (new text content).

Optional: delay_message, delay_typing, mentioned, quoted_id (by ID), type ("text" or "id").
- delay_message: server-side delay (seconds or range like "2-5")
- delay_typing: shows typing indicator for N seconds
- mentioned: array of phones to @mention in groups
- quoted_id: reply to message by exact message ID
- type: "id" (match by ID, default) or "text" (match by text content)

Marks one or more messages as read. Triggers the blue double-check for the sender.

Required: `phone`, `messageIds` (array of message ID strings).

Sends a single call button message. Recipient sees a button that initiates a phone call when tapped.

Required: `phone`, `callPhone`, `button_text`, `title`, `text`.

Optional: delay_message, delay_typing, mentioned, quoted_id (by ID), type ("text" or "id").
- delay_message: server-side delay (seconds or range like "2-5")
- delay_typing: shows typing indicator for N seconds
- mentioned: array of phones to @mention in groups
- quoted_id: reply to message by exact message ID
- type: "id" (match by ID, default) or "text" (match by text content)

Sends an interactive message with up to 5 buttons of mixed types.

Required: `phone`, `message`, `title`, `text`, `buttons`.

Button types (mix & match):
- `quick_reply` — simple tap button with `buttonText` and `id`
- `url` — opens a URL when tapped (`url` field)
- `call` — initiates a phone call (`callPhone` field)
- `otp` — one-time password copy button (`code` field)

⚠ PIX button is NOT supported in combined lists — use /messages/send-button-pix.

Button object example:
`{"type": "quick_reply", "buttonText": "Yes", "id": "yes"}`

Optional: delay_message, delay_typing, mentioned, quoted_id (by ID), type ("text" or "id").
- delay_message: server-side delay (seconds or range like "2-5")
- delay_typing: shows typing indicator for N seconds
- mentioned: array of phones to @mention in groups
- quoted_id: reply to message by exact message ID
- type: "id" (match by ID, default) or "text" (match by text content)

Optional: delay_message, delay_typing, mentioned, quoted_id (by ID), type ("text" or "id").

- type: "id" (match by ID, default) or "text" (match by text content)

Sends a PIX payment button (⚠ exclusive — cannot be combined with other button types).

Required:

- `merchantName` — your business/trading name
- `pixCity` — your city

Optional: `pixDescription`, `button_text`, `title`, `text`.

Optional: delay_message, delay_typing, mentioned, quoted_id (by ID), type ("text" or "id").

query.description: Filter by level: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL

request.description:
Returns server logs with powerful filtering. All filter parameters below are optional query params — use as many or as few as needed.

Date filters (use `date` OR `from_date`+`to_date`, never both):
- `date` (YYYY-MM-DD): Specific day's logs (default: today)
- `from_date` / `to_date` (YYYY-MM-DD): Date range

Time filter (refines within the selected date(s)):
- `from_time` / `to_time` (HH:MM): Time range

Content filters:
- `level`: Log level — `DEBUG`, `INFO`, `WARNING`, `ERROR`, `CRITICAL`
- `search`: Case-insensitive full-text search in log messages

Pagination:
- `limit`: Max lines to return (default: 200, max: 10000)
- `offset`: Skip first N matching lines

Response: `{"success": true, "filters": {...}, "total": N, "returned": N, "offset": N, "logs": [...]}`

Runs log maintenance in two steps:
1. **Compress** — older .log files (not today's) are gzip-compressed to .log.gz
2. **Cleanup** — compressed files are deleted if they exceed age (LOG_MAX_AGE_DAYS, default 30) or total log directory size (LOG_MAX_SIZE_MB, default 50 MB)

Response: `{"success": true, "compressed": N, "deleted": N}`

Lists all available log files with their date, size in bytes, and a human-readable size string. Each file covers one day (zapunlocked_YYYY-MM-DD.log).

Response:
`{"success": true, "count": N, "files": [{"file": "...", "date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "sizeBytes": N, "sizeFormatted": "..."}]}`
Clear Storage.
Manual Cleanup.

Updates the database cleanup configuration.

Required: `interval` — cleanup interval in minutes. Older messages are purged periodically based on this setting.

Root Route.

Returns real-time runtime statistics accumulated since the server started:

- **uptime**: seconds + human-readable uptime string
- **connected**: duration of the current WhatsApp connection (if connected)
- **messages**: total sent/received count
- **webhooks**: total webhook events fired

Response example:
`{"uptime": {"seconds": 3600, "formatted": "1h 0m 0s"}, "connected": {...}, "messages": {"sent": 42, "received": 128}, "webhooks": {"fired": 7}}`

Resets all runtime counters (messages sent/received, webhooks fired) back to zero. The server continues running — only the in-memory + persisted counters are cleared.

Response: `{"success": true}`

Returns per-webhook delivery statistics: how many times each webhook was fired and the timestamp of the last fire.

Response:
`{"total": N, "webhooks": [{"name": "...", "fired": N, "last_fired": "..."}]}`

Resets delivery statistics for ALL webhooks. Each webhook's fired count goes to 0 and last_fired becomes null.

Response: `{"success": true}`

Returns delivery stats for a single webhook by name.

Response: `{"name": "...", "fired": N, "last_fired": "..."}`

Returns 404 if the webhook name has no recorded stats.

Resets delivery statistics for a single webhook by name.

Response: `{"success": true}`

Returns 404 if the webhook name has no recorded stats.
Returns the current server and WhatsApp connection status: client readiness, instance name/ID, and connection state (connected/disconnected/connecting).
Simple liveness check. Returns HTTP 200 immediately regardless of WhatsApp state. Use `/status/readiness` for a deeper check.
Readiness probe. Returns HTTP 200 only after the server has fully initialized and the WhatsApp client is ready. Useful for container orchestration (Kubernetes, Docker).
Returns memory usage from the Node/V8 heap: RSS, heap total, heap used, external memory, and array buffers. Useful for resource monitoring.
Returns the current WhatsApp stream/connection state: `connected`, `disconnected`, `connecting`, etc. Also includes reconnect attempts if applicable.
Returns data usage statistics for the current WhatsApp session, including bytes sent and received.

Updates environment variables at runtime. Changes take effect immediately without requiring a server restart.

Creates a new webhook endpoint. The server will POST event payloads to this URL whenever matching events occur.

Required: `name`, `url` (your endpoint), `events` (array of event names, or `["*"]` for all), `active` (boolean to enable immediately).

Available event types:
- `message.*` / `message.text` / `message.image` / `message.audio` / etc.
- `message.contacts` — contact array messages
- `message.sent` / `message.delivered` / `message.read` — delivery receipts
- `message.poll_created` / `message.poll_vote` — poll events
- `connection.*` — connection state changes
- `qr.*` — QR code events
- `call.*` — call events
- `group.*` — group events
- `contact.*` — presence/chat state events
- `media.cleanup.*` — automatic media cleanup events
- `ai.response` — Meta AI reply received

Creates a new webhook endpoint. The server will POST event payloads to this URL whenever matching events occur.

Required: `name`, `url` (your endpoint), `events` (array of event names, or `["*"]` for all), `active` (boolean to enable immediately).

Available event types:
- `message.*` / `message.text` / `message.image` / `message.audio` / etc.
- `message.contacts` — contact array messages
- `message.sent` / `message.delivered` / `message.read` — delivery receipts
- `message.poll_created` / `message.poll_vote` — poll events
- `connection.*` — connection state changes
- `qr.*` — QR code events
- `call.*` — call events
- `group.*` — group events
- `contact.*` — presence/chat state events
- `media.cleanup.*` — automatic media cleanup events
- `ai.response` — Meta AI reply received
List Events.

Get Webhook.

Path parameters: `name`

Update Webhook.

Path parameters: `name`
Request body: `WebhookUpdate` schema

Delete Webhook.

Path parameters: `name`

Sends a test payload to a registered webhook to verify connectivity and payload format.

The `{name}` path param specifies which webhook to test.

Optional body — specify an `event` to choose which payload template to send. If omitted, defaults to `message.text`.

Available event types for testing:
- `message.text`, `message.image`, `message.video`, `message.audio`
- `message.document`, `message.sticker`, `message.reaction`
- `message.location`, `message.button_reply`, `message.list_reply`
- `message.deleted`, `message.poll_created`, `message.poll_vote`
- `message.sent`, `message.delivered`, `message.read`
- `connection.connected`, `connection.disconnected`, `connection.qr_ready`
- `group.join`, `contact.presence`, `contact.chat_presence`
- `call.received`, `media.cleanup.completed`, `ai.response`

Example body: `{"event": "message.image"}`

Response includes the target's HTTP `statusCode` and `response` body.

Toggle Webhook.

Path parameters: `name`
Request body: `WebhookToggle` schema
